title: Analytics - Device Added
scenario: Analytics pairing event does not display Device Name, Device PID, and Device Serial in Firebase after successful Bluetooth pairing
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. On the Home screen, ensure no device is currently connected.
3. Put the Poly Bluetooth audio device into pairing mode (press and hold the Bluetooth button until the LED flashes).
4. In the Poly Studio Mobile app, tap "Add Device" or select the device from the available devices list on the Home screen.
5. Complete the pairing process by confirming the pairing request on both the app and the device (if required).
6. Verify the device appears as "Connected" on the Home screen.
7. Navigate to the Firebase Analytics console and open the page for the pairing success event.
8. Check that the following default elements are displayed: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by Country/Gender, events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment.
9. Verify that the following custom parameters are also present: Device Name, Device PID, and Device Serial.
10. Wait a few hours after the pairing event, then return to the Firebase Analytics page and check that the event count for the related elements has increased.
expected: The Firebase Analytics page for the pairing success event displays all default elements (graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by Country/Gender, events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment) plus the custom parameters Device Name, Device PID, and Device Serial. After a few hours, the event count for these elements increases.
